about: Per-zone plantar load norms per sport/movement, for flagging OVER/UNDER-used foot regions vs the field. dist_pct = % of total plantar load per zone (~sums 100). peak_force_BW = peak vertical GRF in body weights. Every value carries a confidence flag: 'published' (a value/range stated in a cited paper), 'published_order' (the RANK ordering is from a paper; magnitudes filled to match), or 'estimated' (biomechanically reasonable, no direct source yet). Add sources -> the DB gets more rigid.

sensor_map.note: our 8-FSR rig senses these directly; met2 & met4 are INTERPOLATED from neighbors (met2~mean(met1,met3), met4~mean(met3,met5)).

gym_grf.cite: Biomechanics of landing in gymnasts: a scoping review, Front. Sports Act. Living, 2025
gym_grf.finding: peak vertical GRF 7.1-15.8 x BW; mats & stiff landings raise it

niu2014.cite: Niu et al., 'Peak Vertical GRF during Two-Leg Landing', BioMed Research International, 2014

run_press.cite: Foot orthoses & forefoot peak plantar pressure in running, 2016

run_region.cite: Baropodometric on-field assessment, elite soccer, Sports (MDPI), 2025
run_region.finding: highest at central met heads > 1st met > heel > 1st toe > lateral mets; central forefoot rises walking->running

skate_grf.cite: Landing GRF in figure skaters and non-skaters; ACSM 'Science of Figure Skating: Jumps'
skate_grf.finding: single-leg landing ~5 x BW (some estimates to ~8 x); knee/ankle flexion absorbs

dance_press.cite: Foot deformity & 1st MTP joint plantar pain in DanceSport athletes, Children, 2022
dance_press.finding: 1st-MTP pain -> LESS hallux load, MORE met-head load; heel height raises 1st-met-head pressure

met2_injury.finding: 2nd metatarsal longest/most rigid -> #1 stress-fracture site; base of 2nd met = 'dancer's fracture'

running_rearfoot.injury: 2nd-met stress fracture (over-striders), heel fat-pad; watch central-met overload

running_forefoot.injury: 2nd/3rd-met stress fracture, sesamoiditis (under met1); central mets carry most

gymnastics_landing.note: forefoot contacts first then heel; magnitudes estimated
gymnastics_landing.injury: very high BW impacts -> central-met & heel stress; stiff landings raise force (gym_grf)

figure_skating_landing.movement: single-leg jump landing, back outside edge
figure_skating_landing.note: ball + lateral(outside) edge; in a rigid boot; magnitudes estimated
figure_skating_landing.injury: 60-100 landings/day on one leg -> lateral-column & central-met overload, navicular

ballet_releve.injury: 1st-MTP overload -> hallux valgus / sesamoiditis; if hallux UNDER-loaded, load shifts to met2 (dancer's fracture)

ballet_pointe.note: COG shifts anteriorly onto met shafts (met2_injury); magnitudes estimated
ballet_pointe.injury: base of 2nd met stress fracture ('dancer's fracture'); hallux/1st-ray load extreme
